title: Large Spanish EEG

funding_references.funder_name: Agencia Nacional de Investigacion y Desarrollo (ANID Chile)

description: This dataset comprises 60 EEG recording sessions from 56 healthy Spanish-speaking participants performing speech perception and silent speech production tasks. Participants listened to 30 daily-use Spanish sentences via audio recordings and were instructed to silently repeat each sentence. The study employed a 64-channel EEG system to investigate neural correlates of speech perception, with the goal of developing subject-independent deep neural network models for sentence classification that could enable speech neuroprostheses without requiring patient-specific training data.
methods_description: EEG data were recorded using a 64-channel system during speech perception and silent speech production tasks. The experimental protocol consisted of four segments per trial: rest (5 seconds with fixation cross), perception (4-5 seconds with sentence repetitions), preparation (1 second blank screen), and silent speech production (5 seconds). Thirty Spanish sentences were presented in randomized order across blocks. For subjects S1-S18, perception lasted 4 seconds with 7 repetitions per sentence; for subjects S19 onward, perception was extended to 5 seconds with 6 repetitions per sentence.
